mission.title: Install and Configure Slimfaas on Kubernetes
mission.description: SlimFaas is a lightweight, plug-and-play Function-as-a-Service (FaaS) platform for Kubernetes. It is designed to be fast, simple, and extremely slim, making it an excellent choice for deploying serverless functions in a Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Create a Namespace
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for SlimFaas to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Deploy SlimFaas Components
steps.description: Apply the necessary manifests to deploy SlimFaas.

steps.title: Verify Deployment
steps.description: Check the status of the deployed pods to ensure everything is running smoothly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of SlimFaas will have the pods running in the 'slimfaas' namespace, and you will be able to access the service through port 8080. Additionally, resource limits will be configured as specified.

uninstall.title: Remove SlimFaas Resources
uninstall.description: Delete the SlimFaas components and the namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade SlimFaas
upgrade.description: To upgrade SlimFaas, reapply the latest manifests.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: Check the pod logs for errors using the command: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n slimfaas`.

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description: Ensure that the service is correctly configured and check the service endpoints with: `kubectl get svc -n slimfaas`.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: If pods are in a pending state, verify that your cluster has enough resources (CPU/Memory) available.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with it.
